author: Sardar Ali, Maulvi
title: Ghiza-ul-Arwah
gloss: A book containing sermons enjoining the observance of God's commandments in Al Quran, such as performance of daily prayers, recitation of Al Quran, abhorrence of evil deeds, etc.
notes: 'Food of Souls' — a sermon/exhortation collection on Quranic observance; the edificatory-preaching register.

author: Shahzada Ram
title: Suarg ki Sirhi
gloss: A pamphlet on salvation and how to achieve it from the point of view of an Arya Samajist.
notes: 'Ladder to Heaven' — an explicitly Arya-Samaj soteriology, 2nd ed., from the Arya Steam Press; the constructive (rather than merely polemical) Arya-Samaj religious teaching. Note a Hindu-reform salvation-doctrine text sitting inside the URDU—RELIGION section otherwise dominated by Islamic works — the shared Urdu religious print-sphere across communities.

title: Tahqiq-ur-Raj'at
gloss: A booklet in which the writer tries to prove from Shia traditions that Mehdi, the last Imam from the descendants of Ali, will re-appear towards the end of this world, translated from the Persian original into Urdu by Altaf Husain.
notes: Shia messianism: the return (raj'at) of the Hidden Imam Mahdi, Persian→Urdu — the Twelver-Shia eschatology; closes the Yusufi Press's Ali/Shia cluster. (Mahdist expectation also resonates with the anti-Ahmadi controversy, since the Ahmadiyya claimed a mahdi/messiah.)

author: Amin Chand
title: Desi Hisab ki Kitab
gloss: Arithmetic on native system of accounts for the Third Lower Primary Classes.
publisher: Author, Teacher, Pandori Najran School, Jullundur District
notes: 'Native System of Accounts' arithmetic for primary classes — the persistence of the indigenous (desi/bahi-khata) accounting system taught alongside the colonial school curriculum; a small resistance/retention of vernacular numeracy against metric/English standardization. On-theme for the reification/standardization dynamic. Section '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.

author: Balak Ram, Anad
title: Pahare ma Gur
notes: Multiplication tables (pahare) primer — the mass cheap-arithmetic-primer market (s2-4 are all multiplication tables). '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.

title: Hindi Hindse
notes: Multiplication tables using 'Hindi hindse' (Devanagari/Hindu numerals), 3rd ed. — note the explicit numeral-system marking (Hindi vs the Pocket Hisab's presumably other numerals), an index of the numeral-script politics. '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.

title: Pocket Hisab
publisher: M. Hira Lal and Sons, Book-sellers
notes: 'Pocket Hisab' multiplication tables, 7th ed. — a durable Hira Lal & Sons arithmetic bestseller; English-loan title ('Pocket'). '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.
